channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Meta tests letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings in a bid to appease the EU
item.description: Meta is testing a feature that will let Facebook users browse eBay listings on Facebook Marketplace before being redirected to complete the purchase on eBay. The test, which is being launched to a small group in Germany, France, and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Flying taxis could be carrying passengers in 2025, but hurdles remain
item.description: When he was still a boy making long, tedious trips between his school and his woodsy home in the mountains during the 1980s, JoeBen Bevirt began fantasizing about flying cars that could whisk him to his destination in a matter of minutes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: GlassFish update fixes restart hangs, random 403 responses
item.description: The Eclipse Foundation has released GlassFish 7.0.21, an update of the enterprise Java application server that focuses on fixing a few tenacious bugs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 7+ speedy steps to free up space on your Mac
item.description: Computers get clogged with digital “stuff” over time, and while we all like to think we’re good at managing all that D-detritus, there’s somehow never quite enough time to clean things up.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Robot Lawn Mowers Gaining Traction at CES 2023
item.description: Robot lawn mowers making waves at CES 2023, with companies like Segway and Lymow showcasing their latest models.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: New League of Legends video might have set up Arcane's sequel series, and now I'm even more desperate to see the hit Netflix show's first follow-up
item.description: A new trailer for Riot Games' hugely popular game series appears to act as a prologue to one of Arcane's sequel projects.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vay is expanding its remotely driven rental fleet to over 100 cars in 2025
item.description: Vay, which operates a remotely driven fleet of EVs in Las Vegas, has announced it will have 100 cars by the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Pioneering AI Contract Launched to Detect Fraudulent Practices in Public Tenders
item.description: La Comisión Nacional de los Mercados y la Competencia (CNMC) launches a pioneering contract using artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms to detect potential fraudulent practices in public tenders.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft reveals billion-dollar cloud and AI investment plan in India
item.description: Satya Nadella confirms $3 billion Microsoft investment to help equip India with artificial intelligence skills.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Planes, trains and third-party risks
item.description: Christmas Eve (and Christmas Day) are arguably the most important time-frame for transportation companies. So it was a big deal when an American Airlines system glitch forced the airline to ask the government for a full shutdown on Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hacked Chrome extensions put 2.6 million users at risk of data leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are embedding dangerous code in Chrome extensions to steal your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Demand for AI skills soars, while demand for programming skills falls – O’Reilly report
item.description: It’s perhaps not a surprise, but demand for AI-related skills has soared, according to technology educator O’Reilly, which just released its annual technology trends report. By contrast, interest in programming languages Python and Java has taken a bit of a nosedive, the company reports.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI revolution drives demand for specialized chips, reshaping global markets
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has rapidly transformed the chip industry since its mainstream arrival over the past two years, driving demand for specialized processors, accelerating design innovation, and reshaping global supply chains and markets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to expect from NaaS in 2025
item.description: Enterprises have been slow to adopt NaaS for a number of reasons, including lack of clarity on exactly what NaaS is, lack of a solid business case that NaaS saves money over the long term, and questions around security, visibility, control, and data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities
item.description: The Treasury hack is sparking new questions about how agencies ensure the security of third-party technology service providers. The post Treasury hack: Lawmakers seek more details on scope, third-party vulnerabilities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Turo rentals emerge as common thread in Las Vegas Cybertruck and New Orleans deadly incidents
item.description: Turo faces scrutiny after tragic incidents involving rented vehicles, raising questions about security measures and regulatory frameworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana bill could force kids under 16 off of social media without parental permission
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS--- If State Sen. Mike Bohacek’s (R-Michiana Shores) bill passes next session, social media platforms would need to block kids under 16 from accessing their content if they don’t receive their parents’ permission to use their sites. ”All we’re trying to do is protect kids,” Bohacek said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Adamson, NewsNation

item.title: Graviton progress: 50% of new AWS instances run on Amazon custom silicon
item.description: Half of all instances spun up on Amazon Web Services over the past two years used Graviton, Amazon’s custom silicon built on Arm processor technology, rather than x86 processors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World
